company_snapshot.industry: Specialty Retail — Party Supplies, Crafts, Toys, Education Supplies
company_snapshot.current_search_vendor: None detected — Proprietary redirect-mapping + server-side keyword search (GREENFIELD)
company_snapshot.ecommerce_platform: Custom/Proprietary Java (likely Oracle ATG heritage) + Mirakl Marketplace

executives.name: Steve Mendlik
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: Oriental Trading will be the first company in the celebration, education, and craft retail space to provide a marketplace of this scale to online customers.
executives.relevance: Marketplace vision signals massive catalog expansion — the exact environment where Algolia federated search and dynamic filtering deliver immediate lift

executives.name: Steve Mendlik
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: By creating this robust marketplace, customers can easily get everything they need, at the prices they want and in one place.
executives.relevance: The 'one place' vision is broken by search — NLP query returns 10,631 unfiltered results

executives.name: Brian Moen
executives.title: Chief Marketing and Merchandising Officer
executives.quote: Fun365 is for anyone looking for easy inspiration to add more fun into everyday life. From party ideas and recipes to DIY crafts for adults and kids, our talented team of influencers provide the inspiration, and we give you the tools and supplies to make it a reality. Our unique experience was designed to help our customers save time and money by providing inspirational content in one easy-to-shop place.
executives.relevance: Content-commerce vision requires federated search to surface inspiration alongside products

executives.name: Brian Moen
executives.title: Chief Marketing and Merchandising Officer
executives.quote: As our skeleton décor has grown in popularity, we've more than doubled our exclusive items this year for our customers.
executives.relevance: Exclusive SKUs double annually — they live or die by OTC's own site search

executives.name: Jonathan Staruck
executives.title: SVP/GM, Fun Express & MindWare
executives.quote: 2026 is a big year for us. Alongside exciting new partnerships and products, we're celebrating 8 million Qwirkle games sold and 15 years since winning Spiel des Jahres.
executives.relevance: Staruck leads both Fun Express B2B ($371M) and MindWare — 45+ new SKUs drive catalog expansion

intelligence_signals.text: OTC selected as one of only 3 beta retailers for Amazon's Retail Ad Service — using search queries and viewed categories as ad signals on their own site.
intelligence_signals.relevance: OTC is monetizing search signals via Amazon ads — if underlying search is poor, ad relevance suffers. Algolia fixes the foundation.

intelligence_signals.text: Fun Express named to Inc. 5000 for 2nd consecutive year with 991% revenue growth, serving 40,000+ companies.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Fun Express growing 10x needs dedicated B2B search — 60,000+ SKUs with seasonal allocation and bulk pricing.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Steve Mendlik on CNBC: 'The consumer is feeling the pinch' — consumer price pressure makes every search conversion dollar count.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Price-sensitive consumers abandon on poor search. Algolia's conversion lift directly addresses the pinch.

intelligence_signals.text: ChatGPT sends 50.49% of OTC's referral traffic. AI Chatbots represent 55.52% of all referring industry traffic.
intelligence_signals.relevance: AI discovery shift requires catalog-quality search to convert AI-referred intent.

intelligence_signals.text: OTC is actively bidding on 'party city' in paid search — direct competitor conquest strategy. Paid search is 13.97% of total traffic.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Algolia improves on-site conversion of high-intent intercept arrivals, making paid conquest spend more efficient.

intelligence_signals.text: US-China tariff rates at ~15%+ compress COGS on OTC's value-priced, China-sourced 40,000+ SKU catalog.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Margin pressure makes per-visit conversion improvement more financially compelling.

intelligence_signals.text: Argano has active, ongoing managed services relationship at OTC post-Oracle Cloud ERP/SCM migration. Direct access to CIO Charles Hunsinger.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Warm introduction path via Argano — request intro to CIO Hunsinger via Argano account team.

intelligence_signals.text: Mirakl turned profitable in 2025 ($218M ARR, +23% YoY) and launched Mirakl Nexus agentic commerce platform.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Mirakl Nexus launch creates a natural opening to discuss Algolia federated search across native + marketplace catalog.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Mendlik: 'Oriental Trading will be the first company in the celebration, education, and craft retail space to provide a marketplace of this scale to online customers.'
intelligence_signals.relevance: Marketplace scale ambition without federated search = broken discovery experience.

intelligence_signals.algolia_angle: Live, funded AWS migration contract through July 2026. Algolia's Java client + cloud-hosted indexing integrates directly with this stack. The contractor does the hands-on implementation work.

intelligence_signals.signal: UX/Product design for digital products. Nebraska, hybrid.

findings.title: Zero SAYT/Autocomplete on 40K+ SKU Catalog
findings.expected_behavior: Autocomplete dropdown with product suggestions, category suggestions, and popular searches
findings.actual_behavior: Nothing. No dropdown, no suggestions, no preview. Search is entirely submit-based across 40,000+ SKUs.
findings.impact_stat: Search users convert 2-3x higher than non-searchers
findings.prospect_description: When shoppers click your search bar, they see nothing — no suggestions, no trending items, no guidance across 40,000+ products.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia InstantSearch delivers sub-50ms as-you-type suggestions across products, categories, and content.

findings.title: Zero NLP/Semantic Search Understanding
findings.tested_query: fun activities for kids birthday party under 50
findings.expected_behavior: Filtered results matching birthday party activities, age-appropriate, under $50
findings.actual_behavior: 10,631 unfiltered Party Supplies results. No price constraint, no age filter, no occasion understanding.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of ecommerce sites fail at key search query types
findings.prospect_description: Searching 'fun activities for kids birthday party under 50' shows 10,631 unfiltered results with no price or age filtering.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch interprets natural language queries, extracting price constraints, audience attributes, and intent context.

findings.title: Zero Typo Tolerance — Brand Name Dumps Catalog
findings.expected_behavior: Corrected to 'Oriental Trading' with 'Did you mean' prompt
findings.actual_behavior: Returned entire catalog (18,666 items) as fallback. No correction offered for transposed vowels in brand name.
findings.impact_stat: 69% of sites mishandle misspellings in autocomplete
findings.prospect_description: Misspelling your own brand name ('oreintal trading') shows 18,666 random products instead of correcting the typo.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's built-in typo tolerance handles transpositions, missing characters, and phonetic similarity out of the box.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Double digit increase in conversions across 200,000 SKUs

findings.title: No Federated Search — Content Siloed From Products
findings.expected_behavior: Multi-content-type results combining policy pages and products
findings.actual_behavior: Zero federation. Search returns either a category page, content page, or keyword results — never mixed.
findings.impact_stat: 72% of websites fail user search expectations
findings.prospect_description: Search never shows products and help content together. Content and products are completely siloed.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia federated search indexes products, categories, content pages, and brands in a single query.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Increased conversions and revenue from search

findings.title: Zero Personalization + Static Facets
findings.expected_behavior: Re-ranked results based on browse history; dynamic facets adapting to query context
findings.actual_behavior: Same 12 static filters for every search. Zero behavioral personalization across 3.776M monthly visitors.
findings.impact_stat: 76% of consumers frustrated when experiences are not personalized
findings.prospect_description: Every shopper sees the exact same experience regardless of browsing history or interests.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia AI Personalization re-ranks results based on user affinity profiles. Dynamic Faceting surfaces context-relevant filters.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Doubled sales year-over-year for 3 consecutive years

findings.title: Full Page Reload Per Search
findings.actual_behavior: Every search triggers a full server-side page reload (800ms–1500ms). No client-side search technology.
findings.impact_stat: Advanced search delivers up to 30% higher conversion vs basic search
findings.prospect_description: Every search requires a full page reload taking 1–2 seconds.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's distributed search network delivers results in <50ms globally. InstantSearch provides as-you-type results without page reloads.

findings.title: Portfolio Brand Returns Zero Results
findings.expected_behavior: Brand page or filtered results for OTC-owned Morris Costumes brand
findings.actual_behavior: ZERO results — homepage fallback. Redirect table not updated for 2021 acquisition.
findings.impact_stat: 13-15% of visitors use search, generating 31-45% of revenue
findings.prospect_description: Your own brand 'Morris Costumes' (acquired 2021) returns zero search results.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules engine with brand detection automatically surfaces brand-specific results without manual redirect updates.

findings.title: No Dynamic Rules Engine for Merchandising
findings.expected_behavior: Dynamic in-SERP merchandising with promotional banners and boosted products
findings.actual_behavior: 5 PDP rec widgets exist (proprietary). Campaign pages via redirect only. No in-SERP merchandising or rules engine.
findings.prospect_description: Merchandising team cannot dynamically promote products in search results. Every campaign requires engineering changes.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules + Merchandising Studio enables marketing teams to configure search merchandising without engineering.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 65% issues automatically solved with dynamic re-ranking

findings.title: Analytics-Rich Stack With Zero Search Intelligence
findings.expected_behavior: Trending badges, bestseller tags, analytics-driven signals in search UX
findings.actual_behavior: 4 analytics vendors but zero analytics signals surface in search. No trending, no bestsellers, no search intelligence.
findings.prospect_description: You invest in 4 analytics platforms but none feeds back into your search experience.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Analytics surfaces search metrics in a merchandiser dashboard, enabling data-driven search optimization.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Improved click-through rate with AI-powered search analytics

moderate.scenario_desc: 6-component model at $75 AOV placeholder: C1 ($10.2M) + C2 ($1.9M) + C3 ($765K) + C4 ($637K) + C5 ($6.0M) + C6 ($1.9M). Replace AOV with actual number — every $25 AOV change scales the total by ~40%.

roi_components.label: Conversion Lift
roi_components.gap_detail: "Black party supplies" does not auto-apply color filter. Morris Costumes (OTC-owned brand) returns ZERO results. Brand intent only works for pre-configured redirects.
roi_components.benchmark: BIG W: +7% search conversion (general merchandise, large catalog — closest OTC peer) [FACT]

roi_components.label: AOV Increase
roi_components.gap_detail: Zero behavioral personalization. Static facets — same 12 filters for costumes, crafts, and party supplies. No AI-driven ranking, no in-SERP promotional banners.
roi_components.benchmark: BIG W: +4.7% basket size. Staples Canada: double-digit conversion lift [FACT]

roi_components.label: Bounce Reduction
roi_components.gap_detail: Zero autocomplete — every search triggers a full server-side page reload (800ms–1500ms). Empty state shows nothing. No-results page is static, non-contextual.
roi_components.benchmark: Lacoste: -88% bounce rate after Algolia deployment — same Solr-era architecture as OTC [FACT]

roi_components.label: No-Results Recovery
roi_components.gap_detail: "easer bunny costume" — typo "easer" ignored, no "Did you mean". "oreintal trading" returns entire 18,666-item catalog as fallback. Morris Costumes brand = zero results.
roi_components.benchmark: Staples Canada: 65% of search issues automatically solved with Algolia [FACT]

roi_components.label: Speed / Latency
roi_components.gap_detail: Full page reload on every search submit — no client-side search. 800ms–1500ms round-trip per query confirmed in browser audit. 8–15× slower than Algolia's <100ms target.
roi_components.benchmark: Amazon: 100ms latency = 1% revenue loss. Lacoste on Algolia: results in <50ms [FACT]

roi_components.label: Long-Tail NLP Discovery
roi_components.gap_detail: "fun activities for kids birthday party under 50" — 10,631 unfiltered results, no price constraint, no occasion understanding. Pure keyword matching on 40,000+ SKU catalog + Mirakl marketplace.
roi_components.benchmark: BIG W: 'blue socks get blue socks' — NLP/relevance was the biggest impact. Dawn Foods: search volume grew from <1M to ~20M searches in 2 years [FACT]

objection_counters.objection: We are in the middle of an AWS migration.
objection_counters.counter: Algolia is cloud-hosted and API-first — it eliminates search from your migration scope. Java SDK integrates in days.

objection_counters.objection: Our redirect-mapping search works well enough.
objection_counters.counter: It works for ~200–300 pre-mapped terms. But Morris Costumes returns zero results, NLP queries return 10,631 unfiltered items, and you have zero typo tolerance.
Those tell you what happened after search. Algolia Analytics tells you which queries failed and which convert — then you act on it in real time.

hiring.build_vs_buy: No search-specific roles posted. Building search in-house requires 3–5 FTEs ($500K–$800K/yr); Algolia starts at $120K/yr.

strategic_angles.label: GREENFIELD: No Search Vendor — $400M+ Addressable Revenue Base
strategic_angles.hook: There is no search vendor here to displace. You get a clean implementation with no migration debt, no political baggage, and a clear ROI baseline measured against your own current conversion rate.
pain_points: GREENFIELD status confirmed — zero third-party search vendor in 182KB of BuiltWith data; proprietary Java .fltr platform
Fun Express B2B: $371M revenue base with 60,000+ SKU catalog
Total addressable search revenue base: $400M–$600M across all digital properties
strategic_angles.discovery_question: With no existing search vendor to migrate away from, how is your team thinking about the build-vs-buy decision for search during the current AWS migration — and who owns that platform choice?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia impact at Lacoste-level lift (+37% CVR, +150% sales from search) on a clean GREENFIELD implementation. [FACT — 04-competitors.md; algolia.com/customers/lacoste, 2026-04-14]
strategic_angles.objection: We are in the middle of an AWS migration.
strategic_angles.objection_counter: Algolia is cloud-hosted and API-first — it eliminates search from your migration checklist rather than adding to it. The Java client integrates in days. Build the right search architecture during the migration, not after.
strategic_angles.source: [FACT — 02-tech-stack.md (GREENFIELD classification, .fltr pattern, no vendor detected); ESTIMATE — 08-financial-profile.md (revenue base, ecdb.com 2026-04-14); FACT — 04-competitors.md (Lacoste case study)]

strategic_angles.label: OTC Is Paying Amazon to Monetize a Broken Search Foundation
strategic_angles.hook: You committed to monetizing search. Algolia makes sure you're not monetizing a broken foundation.
pain_points: OTC selected as one of only three beta retailers for Amazon Retail Ad Service — on-site search advertising using query signals
Amazon's ad service is only as good as the search engine underneath it — poor NLP and zero typo tolerance degrade ad placement
Wasted impressions and underperforming ad revenue when search misfires on shopper intent
strategic_angles.discovery_question: You were selected by Amazon as one of three beta partners to monetize on-site search. When you evaluate the ROI on that investment, how confident are you that the search foundation underneath it is returning relevant results for the queries driving those ad placements?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch fixes the query understanding gap — so every search that Amazon's ad service monetizes returns a relevant result. [FACT — 02-tech-stack.md; 09-browser-findings.md]
strategic_angles.objection: Our redirect-mapping search works well enough.
strategic_angles.objection_counter: It works for ~200–300 pre-mapped terms. But Morris Costumes returns zero results, 'easer bunny costume' ignores the typo, and 'fun activities for kids birthday party under 50' returns 10,631 unfiltered products. That is the search Amazon is being paid to monetize.
strategic_angles.source: [FACT — 09b-social-signals.md (Amazon Retail Ad Service beta, Signal 1, score 9/10, Marketing Dive + advertising.amazon.com, 2026-04-14/2025-01-09)]

strategic_angles.label: The B2B Arm Is OTC's Growth Engine — and It Has No Search
strategic_angles.hook: Your B2B arm is growing 10x. Is its search experience scaling with it? Fun Express's 60,000-SKU catalog running without purpose-built B2B search is the fastest-growing untapped Algolia beachhead in this account.
pain_points: Fun Express: Inc. 5000 for second consecutive year with 991% revenue growth
60,000+ SKUs; B2B buyers search by SKU code, quantity threshold, price band — none supported by generic Java platform
257K monthly visits vs. orientaltrading.com's 3.8M — severely underdeveloped
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Fun Express is growing 10x year-over-year. Is the search experience on funexpress.com built for B2B bulk ordering workflows, or does it share the same B2C stack as orientaltrading.com?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Dawn Foods (Algolia B2B customer): 10,000+ SKU catalog, time-to-order fell 30% in four years, sales doubled year-over-year for three consecutive years. Fun Express maps directly. [FACT — 04-competitors.md; algolia.com/customers/dawnfoods, 2026-04-14]
strategic_angles.objection_counter: Those tell you what happened after search. Algolia Analytics tells you which queries failed, which convert, and which are returning zero results — then you act on it in real time. That is what you need to scale Fun Express search.
strategic_angles.source: [FACT — 09b-social-signals.md (Fun Express Inc. 5000, Signal 2, score 8/10, PRWeb 2025-08-18); FACT — 04-competitors.md (Dawn Foods case study, algolia.com/customers/dawnfoods 2026-04-14)]

strategic_angles.label: 50%+ of Exclusive SKUs Are Invisible Everywhere Except OTC's Own Search
strategic_angles.hook: Your exclusive SKUs cannot be found anywhere else. When your search fails on them, the sale vanishes completely. There is no fallback channel.
pain_points: Over 50% of OTC's 40,000+ SKUs are exclusive — not on Amazon, Google Shopping, or any competitor
CMO Brian Moen: 'As our skeleton décor has grown in popularity, we've more than doubled our exclusive items this year'
Zero-results or poor ranking on an exclusive SKU = lost sale with no fallback — cannot be found elsewhere
strategic_angles.discovery_question: More than half your catalog is exclusive to Oriental Trading. When your search fails on an exclusive SKU — wrong ranking, typo mismatch, or zero results — where does that customer go, and what happens to the sale?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia typo tolerance, NLP semantic search, and AI ranking ensure exclusive SKUs surface correctly for every search variation — 'skeleton decor', 'skelton decorations', 'Halloween skeleton party'. [FACT — 09-browser-findings.md]
strategic_angles.objection: Our redirect-mapping search works well enough.
strategic_angles.objection_counter: It works for the ~200–300 terms you've manually mapped. It cannot handle typos, synonyms, or long-tail queries on your exclusive catalog. An exclusive SKU that returns zero results is a lost sale with nowhere for the customer to go.
strategic_angles.source: [FACT — 11-investor-intelligence.md (Moen Quote 6, PRWeb September 2019); FACT — 01-company-context.md (40,000+ SKUs, >50% exclusive); FACT — 08-financial-profile.md (ecdb.com channel data, 2026-04-14)]

strategic_angles.label: Mirakl Nexus AI Launch: Federated Search Is Now Mandatory
strategic_angles.hook: Your Mirakl partner just launched an AI platform. Algolia's federated search is the missing piece that makes Nexus AI actionable — unified discovery across your 40,000 owned SKUs and millions of marketplace products in a single experience.
pain_points: OTC launched Mirakl marketplace June 2021 — millions of marketplace products alongside 40,000 first-party SKUs
Mirakl announced profitability ($218M ARR, +23% YoY) and launched Mirakl Nexus agentic commerce platform — February 2026
OTC search does not federate across 1P and marketplace SKUs — marketplace is a catalog island
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Mirakl just launched Nexus AI. When a shopper searches for 'Halloween party favors bulk' on orientaltrading.com, are they seeing results from both your first-party catalog and your Mirakl marketplace sellers in a single ranked result set — or are those two separate discovery surfaces?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia federated search unifies first-party and Mirakl marketplace SKUs in a single ranked, filtered, relevant result set — the enabling layer for Nexus AI adoption. [FACT — 02-tech-stack.md; 04-competitors.md]
strategic_angles.objection: We are in the middle of an AWS migration.
strategic_angles.objection_counter: Algolia's API-first architecture integrates independently of the migration timeline. You can federate Mirakl marketplace search now — it does not require the AWS migration to complete first.
strategic_angles.source: [FACT — 09c-news-signals.md (Mirakl Nexus launch, Digital Commerce 360, 2026-02-27); FACT — 11-investor-intelligence.md (Mendlik Quote 1, BusinessWire Mirakl launch 2021); FACT — 02-tech-stack.md (Mirakl SaaS in stack, 2026-04-14)]

priority_to_product.question: How are shoppers discovering marketplace products today?
What is your search conversion rate and zero-results rate?
How quickly can your team update search for a new campaign?
Does Fun Express share the B2C search stack?

anchor_lines: Your CEO said customers should 'easily get everything in one place' — NLP queries return 10,631 unfiltered results.
You're investing in Amazon Retail Ad Service — but the search underneath has zero typo tolerance and zero NLP.
Fun Express is growing 991% — Dawn Foods (B2B, 10K+ SKUs) doubled sales 3 years running on Algolia.

positioning_statements.timing: AWS migration active through July 2026 + post-Easter peak + Amazon Retail Ad Service beta = ideal timing for search modernization.

golden_angle.detail: No competitor confirmed on Algolia. First-mover advantage in AI-powered search for party/craft/value specialty retail.
golden_angle.talk_track: While Party City runs SFCC native search and Shindigz runs 2010-era Solr, Algolia gives OTC the same search infrastructure as Staples Canada (200K SKUs, double-digit conversion lift) and Walgreens (one of the fastest Algolia implementations in enterprise retail).

Dawn Foods: doubled sales YoY for 3 years (B2B)

methodology: Systematic browser testing of orientaltrading.com using 16 targeted queries across 7 categories. Network traffic monitored to confirm search vendor status. 26 screenshots captured. Scoring uses 10-area framework with weighted severity (HIGH=2x, MEDIUM=1x, LOW=0.5x). Traffic from SimilarWeb (Jan–Mar 2026). Financials from ecdb.com, ZoomInfo, trade press. Executive quotes from BusinessWire, PRWeb, CNBC.

key_benchmarks.context: Three-quarters of consumers express frustration when their shopping experiences are not personalized, while 71% expect personalization from brands and 91% prefer brands that provide relevant recommendations — making personalized search a baseline expectation in specialty retail.

key_benchmarks.context: Search users represent high-intent shoppers who already know what they want — making search quality the single highest-leverage conversion lever for catalog-heavy retailers like Oriental Trading with 40,000+ SKUs.
key_benchmarks.label: [FACT — Klevu/AddSearch via WebFetch, 2025-06-15, https://www.opensend.com/post/on-site-search-conversion-rate-statistics-ecommerce]

touches.subject: Email 1 — Hook
touches.body:
# Email 1 — Hook

**To:** [Name], [Title] — Oriental Trading Company
**From:** [AE Name], Algolia
**Day:** Day 1

---

**Subject:** "Morris Costumes" on orientaltrading.com returns zero results

---

**Body:**

[First name] —

OTC's 2021 acquisition of Morris Costumes expanded your portfolio into theatrical and Halloween — but your own site search doesn't know the brand exists.

I tested it last week: searching "Morris Costumes" on orientaltrading.com returns zero results and falls back to the homepage, as if the brand never existed. Meanwhile, 71% of your traffic arrives on mobile, where shoppers type brand names and expect instant answers.

With your 40,000+ SKU catalog expanding through Mirakl and brand acquisitions, a redirect-table-only search engine can't keep pace — every new brand addition creates a new discovery gap.

Worth a 15-minute conversation to walk through what we found?

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**

- Finding: `09-browser-findings.md` — Step 2i (Intent Detection Test), lines 92–98
- Screenshot evidence: `deliverables/screenshots/09-intent-morris-costumes.png` — confirms zero results for "Morris Costumes" query
- Morris Costumes acquisition: `08-financial-profile.md` lines 99, 159 — acquired March 2021, ~$50M
- Mobile traffic (71%): `09-browser-findings.md` — Step 2l (Mobile Experience), confirmed via playbook MEDDPICC section ("71% mobile traffic")
- Catalog size (40,000+ SKUs): `oriental-trading-playbook.md` — BLUF section and Discovery Questions
- Mirakl marketplace: `11-investor-intelligence.md` Signal 2 — "Marketplace Scale = Search Crisis"

touches.subject: Email 2 — Competitor
touches.body:
# Email 2 — Competitor

**To:** [Name], [Title] — Oriental Trading Company
**From:** [AE Name], Algolia
**Day:** Day 3–4

---

**Subject:** While Party City runs SFCC's native search, here's the gap OTC could own

---

**Body:**

[First name] —

Party City — your most direct competitor — runs Salesforce Einstein Search, which is bundled into their Commerce Cloud platform. It's constrained, non-semantic, and limited by the platform it lives inside. They also filed Chapter 11 in January 2024, which means their search investment is frozen.

Shindigz, your second major competitor in personalized party supplies, runs Apache Solr — infrastructure from 2010. No AI ranking, no NLP, no personalization.

Neither of them has deployed a dedicated search platform.

That's the window. Staples Canada — a B2B+B2C hybrid with 200,000 SKUs (five times OTC's core catalog) — deployed Algolia and saw a double-digit increase in conversions. Their catalog complexity is analogous to OTC's. Their implementation took 6 months.

First-mover in AI-powered search within party and craft specialty retail is still available. Happy to show you what that looks like for your specific catalog and your Fun Express B2B channel.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**

**Competitive intelligence — DEFENSIVE play used (no direct competitor confirmed on Algolia):**
- Party City platform (Salesforce Commerce Cloud) and search (Einstein Search): `04-competitors.md` lines 19, 35–38 — [FACT — AfterShip + WebSearch, 2026-04-14]
- Party City Chapter 11: `04-competitors.md` line 36 — [FACT]
- Shindigz platform (Kibo/Apache Solr): `04-competitors.md` lines 20, 43–46 — [FACT — Echidna case study + Kibo docs, 2026-04-14]; Solr 9 migration June 2024
- No competitor using Algolia confirmed: `04-competitors.md` Golden Angle Assessment section — "OFFENSIVE — no direct competitor confirmed on Algolia"
- Staples Canada case study: `04-competitors.md` lines 110–122 — [FACT — algolia.com/customers/staples WebFetch, 2026-04-14]; 200,000 SKUs, double-digit conversion increase, B2B+B2C model
- Fun Express B2B angle: `08-financial-profile.md` Fun Express section — $371M B2B segment [ESTIMATE — ZoomInfo]
- Golden Angle status: Not applicable — Defensive play used per skill rules because no direct competitor uses Algolia

touches.subject: LinkedIn — Follow-Up 1
touches.body:
# LinkedIn — Follow-Up 1

**Timing:** 3–5 days after connection accepted, no reply received
**Goal:** Build credibility. Share the specific finding. No ask.

---

## Message (for CIO / Technical Buyer)

Thanks for connecting. The finding I mentioned: when a shopper types "Morris Costumes" on orientaltrading.com, they get zero results and land on the homepage.

Here's the deeper context: OTC's search is built on a redirect-mapping architecture — roughly 200–300 terms manually mapped to category pages. MindWare (acquired 2013) maps correctly. Morris Costumes (acquired 2021) was never added.

With your AWS migration underway and Mirakl expanding the catalog to millions of SKUs, every new brand addition creates a new discovery gap in the redirect table.

No ask here — just thought you'd want to see what we found.

**Character count: 492** ✓ (under 500 limit)

---

## Message (for CMO / Marketing Buyer)

Thanks for connecting. One more data point from the audit: I searched "fun activities for kids birthday party under 50" — a query your target shopper types every day in April and May.

Result: 10,631 unfiltered Party Supplies. No price constraint applied. No age filter. No occasion targeting. Just a firehose of products.

Your CEO said in May 2024 that "the consumer is feeling the pinch." When a price-sensitive shopper gets 10,000 results with no price filter, they don't scroll — they leave for Amazon.

Sharing because the data is compelling. No ask.

**Character count: 487** ✓ (under 500 limit)

---

**Source notes:**

- Morris Costumes finding: `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2i — zero results; screenshot `09-intent-morris-costumes.png`
- Redirect architecture: `09-browser-findings.md` Search Architecture Assessment section
- MindWare comparison: `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2i — "MindWare...correctly maps to its brand page via redirect"
- AWS migration: `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Recent News section — Java dev contract through July 2026
- Mirakl catalog scale: `11-investor-intelligence.md` Signal 2 — "millions of products"
- NLP query finding: `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2m — screenshot `13-nlp-kids-birthday.png`
- CEO "feeling the pinch" quote: `11-investor-intelligence.md` Signal 4 — [ESTIMATE — CNBC Squawk Box May 3, 2024]; used as ESTIMATE per source labeling
- No sales ask in Follow-up 1 per skill rules

touches.subject: Email 3 — Business Case
touches.body:
# Email 3 — Business Case

**To:** [Name], [Title] — Oriental Trading Company
**From:** [AE Name], Algolia
**Day:** Day 7

---

**Subject:** $197M e-commerce, 3.5% conversion — one number worth stress-testing

---

**Body:**

[First name] —

orientaltrading.com generated $197M in e-commerce revenue in 2025 at a 3.5–4.0% conversion rate.

Here's the math worth stress-testing: search users convert at 2–3x the rate of non-searchers. If OTC's search converts at or below the sitewide average — because it returns zero results for brand names, delivers 10,631 unfiltered results for NLP queries, and provides zero guidance to mobile users typing in the search bar — the gap between where you are and where you could be is measurable.

One component: a 0.5 percentage-point improvement in conversion across the $197M base = $1.4M in additional annual revenue. Algolia's annual cost for a catalog of OTC's size typically runs $120K–$800K depending on scope.

That's a 2–10x return before you factor in Fun Express B2B, where search-influenced purchasing on a $371M revenue base moves a very different needle.

I can send the full business case model as a follow-up, or walk through it live in 15 minutes. Either works.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**

- E-commerce revenue ($197M, 2025): `08-financial-profile.md` Source 1 (ecdb.com section) — [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com WebFetch, April 2026]
- Conversion rate (3.5–4.0%): `08-financial-profile.md` Source 1 — [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com WebFetch, April 2026]
- Search user conversion multiplier (2–3x): `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Audit Highlights, Finding 1 — citing Forrester/Mailmodo via opensend.com
- Zero results for "Morris Costumes": `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2i, screenshot `09-intent-morris-costumes.png`
- 10,631 results for NLP query: `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2m — "fun activities for kids birthday party under 50" → 10,631 Party Supplies
- Zero SAYT for mobile users: `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2c — "CRITICAL — Zero SAYT/autocomplete capability"
- $1.4M incremental revenue calculation: `08-financial-profile.md` Algolia Search Revenue Opportunity section — 0.5 pp improvement × $197M = $1.4M [ESTIMATE — derived calculation, April 2026]
- Algolia cost range ($120K–$800K): `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Pilot Strategy section
- Fun Express revenue ($371M): `08-financial-profile.md` Fun Express section — [ESTIMATE — ZoomInfo, April 2026]
- Leave-behind: `deliverables/oriental-trading-leave-behind.html` — available at this path

touches.subject: Loom Video Script — Oriental Trading Company
touches.body:
# Loom Video Script — Oriental Trading Company

**AE Guidance:** Record this as a personal screen-share video. Open orientaltrading.com in a browser tab and the screenshots folder in a second window. 2-minute max. Casual, direct — not a sales pitch.

**Total estimated word count:** 248 words (comfortable 2-minute delivery at 120–130 wpm)

---

## HOOK (0–10 sec)

"I spent 30 minutes on orientaltrading.com last week and found three things that surprised me — especially given where they are with their AWS migration and marketplace expansion. Let me show you."

*[Speaker note: Open to the orientaltrading.com homepage as you say this. Screen should be live, not a recording of a recording.]*

---

## DEMO — Finding 1 (10–40 sec)

*[Display screenshot: `03-sayt-party.png`]*

"This is what happens when a shopper types 'party' in the search bar on a catalog with 40,000 products. Nothing. No suggestions, no product previews, no category hints — just a blank text field waiting for them to press Enter. On mobile, which is 71% of their traffic, this is the entire search experience before submit."

*[Speaker note: Pause briefly here. Let the silence land.]*

"Every search begins in the dark."

---

## DEMO — Finding 2 (40–75 sec)

*[Display screenshot: `13-nlp-kids-birthday.png`]*

"This is a shopper typing 'fun activities for kids birthday party under 50' — a completely normal April search query. They get 10,631 Party Supplies results. No price filter applied from 'under 50.' No age filter from 'kids.' No occasion ranking from 'birthday party.' Just the entire catalog, dumped.

That shopper's next click is Amazon.

OTC's search doesn't understand natural language — it treats every query as a bag of keywords, not a question."

*[Speaker note: This is your strongest finding. Deliver it at normal pace — don't rush.]*

---

## DEMO — Finding 3 (75–105 sec)

*[Display screenshot: `09-intent-morris-costumes.png`]*

"Last one. OTC acquired Morris Costumes in 2021. If you search that brand name on their own website today, you get zero results and fall back to the homepage.

Their search is built on a redirect-mapping table — a manually maintained list of maybe 200 terms. MindWare, acquired in 2013, is in the table. Morris Costumes, acquired three years ago, is not.

With Mirakl expanding their catalog to millions of SKUs, every new brand addition creates a new discovery gap."

*[Speaker note: Cut to your face briefly here before the close.]*

---

## CTA (105–120 sec)

"Three gaps. On a $197M e-commerce business at 3.5% conversion, even a half-point lift from better search is $1.4 million per year.

I put together a full analysis — every finding, the revenue math, and how a Party Supplies pilot would work. Happy to walk through it in 15 minutes.

[Your name], [Title], Algolia."

*[Speaker note: End on your name and title. Keep it warm, not salesy.]*

---

**Screenshot verification (all 3 references confirmed on disk):**
- `03-sayt-party.png` — VERIFIED: exists in `deliverables/screenshots/`
- `13-nlp-kids-birthday.png` — VERIFIED: exists in `deliverables/screenshots/`
- `09-intent-morris-costumes.png` — VERIFIED: exists in `deliverables/screenshots/`

**Source notes:**

- Finding 1 (zero SAYT): `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2c — screenshot `03-sayt-party.png`; 71% mobile traffic from playbook MEDDPICC section
- Finding 2 (NLP failure): `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2m — screenshot `13-nlp-kids-birthday.png`; "10,631 Party Supplies + thousands more"
- Finding 3 (Morris Costumes): `09-browser-findings.md` Step 2i — screenshot `09-intent-morris-costumes.png`; redirect table architecture from Search Architecture Assessment section
- Revenue figure ($197M, 3.5%, $1.4M): `08-financial-profile.md` Source 1 (ecdb.com) and Algolia Search Revenue Opportunity section — [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com WebFetch, April 2026]
- Mirakl catalog expansion: `11-investor-intelligence.md` Signal 2 — [FACT — BusinessWire WebFetch, 2026-04-14]

# LinkedIn — Follow-Up 2

**Timing:** 1 week after Follow-Up 1, no reply
**Goal:** Move toward a meeting with a soft ask.

---

## Message (Competitor angle — for CIO / Technical Buyer)

One more data point worth sharing.

Staples Canada — B2B+B2C hybrid, 200,000 SKUs, two buyer channels — deployed Algolia and saw a double-digit increase in conversions. Catalog complexity similar to OTC's core + Fun Express combined.

Neither Party City (SFCC Einstein) nor Shindigz (Apache Solr) has a dedicated search platform. The first-mover window in your vertical is still open.

Would a 15-minute conversation make sense? Happy to walk through what a Party Supplies pilot would look like at OTC's scale.

**Character count: 438** ✓ (under 500 limit)

---

## Message (Business case angle — for CMO / Marketing Buyer)

Last thought, then I'll leave you alone.

orientaltrading.com: $197M e-commerce, 3.5% conversion. A 0.5 percentage-point improvement from better search = $1.4M per year. Algolia's cost for a catalog at OTC's scale: $120K–$800K depending on scope.

Steve Mendlik's vision — "customers can easily get everything they need, at the prices they want and in one place" — requires search that actually understands what they're looking for.

Would it make sense to spend 15 minutes on what that looks like?

**Character count: 431** ✓ (under 500 limit)

---

**Source notes:**

- Competitor angle: `04-competitors.md` — Staples Canada (FACT, lines 110–122), Party City/SFCC, Shindigz/Solr
- First-mover framing: `04-competitors.md` Golden Angle Assessment — "OFFENSIVE — no direct competitor confirmed on Algolia"

touches.body:
# Email 4 — Social Proof

**To:** [Name], [Title] — Oriental Trading Company
**From:** [AE Name], Algolia
**Day:** Day 14

---

**Subject:** Staples Canada, 200K SKUs, double-digit conversion lift — your model, their result

---

**Body:**

[First name] —

Staples Canada runs a B2B+B2C hybrid model with 200,000 SKUs across Staples.ca (consumer) and Staplespreferred.ca (business). It's a near-direct structural parallel to OTC's orientaltrading.com (B2C) and Fun Express (B2B) — two channels, two buyer types, one catalog.

Their gap before Algolia: relevance and ranking that couldn't scale with catalog growth. Sound familiar?

After deploying Algolia's Dynamic Re-Ranking, Dynamic Synonym Suggestions, and AI Search, Staples Canada saw a double-digit increase in conversions and 65% of search issues resolved automatically — without manual intervention.

Their principal product manager called it: "What really worked miracles for us was the dynamic re-ranking algorithm — that was a real game changer."

OTC has the same catalog complexity and a structurally weaker search baseline. The lift opportunity is larger, not smaller.

Happy to walk through how we'd scope a pilot on OTC's Party Supplies category — the highest-traffic, highest-seasonality category on your site. 15 minutes.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**

- Staples Canada case study: `04-competitors.md` lines 110–122 — [FACT — algolia.com/customers/staples WebFetch, 2026-04-14]
- URL verified: https://www.algolia.com/customers/staples/
- Metrics: "Double Digit increase in conversions," "65% Issues automatically solved," "200,000 SKUs" — verbatim from Algolia case study page [FACT]
- Executive quote (Diptendu Ray, Principal Product Manager, Staples Canada): `04-competitors.md` line 118 — [FACT — algolia.com/customers/staples WebFetch, 2026-04-14]
- B2B+B2C model parallel: `04-competitors.md` lines 111–113 — "directly parallel to OTC's B2C + B2B Fun Express model"
- Fun Express B2B: `08-financial-profile.md` Fun Express section — $371M [ESTIMATE — ZoomInfo, April 2026]
- Party Supplies pilot recommendation: `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Pilot Strategy section
- ICP vertical (multi-category specialty retail): `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Competitive Context section

touches.body:
# Email 5 — Break-Up

**To:** [Name], [Title] — Oriental Trading Company
**From:** [AE Name], Algolia
**Day:** Day 21

---

**Subject:** Closing the loop on Oriental Trading

---

**Body:**

[First name] —

I know this landed during a busy stretch — AWS migration, post-Easter recovery, Amazon Retail Ad Service rollout. Timing isn't always right.

I'm going to stop following up, but I wanted to leave one thing behind: a full audit of orientaltrading.com's search experience — what we found, what the gaps mean, and what the revenue math looks like at your scale.

You can find it here: https://algolia-arian-v2.vercel.app/oriental-trading/index.html

If search makes it onto your radar in Q3 or Q4, I'm an easy person to reach. This work will still be here.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia
[Email] | [Phone]

---

**Source notes:**

- Timing references (AWS migration, Amazon Retail Ad Service): `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Recent News & Trigger Events section — Java developer contract through July 2026 (Appcast), Amazon Retail Ad Service beta (Marketing Dive, Jan 2025)
- Post-Easter traffic peak: `oriental-trading-playbook.md` Recent News section — "+23.9% MoM in March" (SimilarWeb)
- Leave-behind resource: `deliverables/oriental-trading-leave-behind.html` — confirmed exists in deliverables directory
- SPA link: per AGENT-CONTEXT.md path convention — Vercel URL pattern: https://algolia-arian-v2.vercel.app/{slug}/index.html
- No "last chance" language used per skill rules. No urgency fabrication.
